title: After 7 e-bike incidents in 10 days, Mass. police chief warns of impending ‘tragedy’
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Milford police reported seven e-bike incidents in 10 days, urging safety education to prevent potential tragedies.
A 15-year-old boy collided with a car on Highlight Street after losing control of his e-bike; no injuries occurred.
E-bikes with motors under 750 watts require no license in Massachusetts, but helmets and age restrictions apply.

title: Bridgewater police warn of Tom Brady impersonation scam
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Bridgewater police warn of scam involving impersonation of Tom Brady targeting local family with gift card requests.
Victim unknowingly sent gift cards to scammer posing as former Patriots quarterback, reported on March 23, 2026.
FTC advises against sending money or gift cards to unknown individuals and being wary of unexpected messages.

title: This Boston Marathon runner is supporting children in their hardest times
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Maria Castaneda, 52, from Cambridge, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for The Children's Room charity.
Castaneda, originally from Mexico, appreciates the Boston community's kindness and supports The Children's Room mission.
The Children's Room provides support to children and families facing grief and difficult times with understanding and compassion.

title: Review and setlist: Lady Gaga’s Mayhem Ball brought the spectacle, but Boston brought the heart
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Lady Gaga performed her Mayhem Ball tour at TD Garden in Boston, delivering a three-hour show of hits and new songs.
Fans organized a surprise tribute with 20,000 white signs, celebrating Gaga's 40th birthday and leaving her visibly emotional.
The setlist included classics like 'Poker Face' and 'Bad Romance' alongside new tracks from Gaga's 'Mayhem' album.

title: Would you try the new couch-style seating on United? Here’s what readers said.
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: United Airlines plans to introduce couch-style seating, called Relax Row, in economy class by 2027.
In a poll, 61% of over 100 respondents look forward to trying the new seating, while 15% are opposed.
Opinions varied; some praised potential comfort, while others criticized possible noise and costs.

title: This first grade teacher is running the Boston Marathon to support her students
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Siobhan Salani, a first grade teacher from Weymouth, is running the Boston Marathon representing the Corey C. Griffin Foundation.
The Corey C. Griffin Foundation supports students at Saint John Paul II Catholic Academy Lower Mills, where Salani teaches.
Salani feels honored to run for the foundation, highlighting its positive impact on Boston children and her students.

title: A Maine beach is among the best in the U.S. in 2026, according to Conde Nast Traveler
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Conde Nast Traveler named Sand Beach at Acadia National Park among America's best beaches for 2026.
Sand Beach is located between granite cliffs on Mount Desert Island with pine-covered scenery and cold Atlantic waters.
Accommodations nearby include Under Canvas Acadia and The Asticou Hotel, noted by major publications for uniqueness.

title: 16-year-old arrested in Allston shooting
author: John Waller
bullets: A 16-year-old was arrested for a shooting in Allston, injuring another juvenile near 222 Brighton Ave last Thursday.
The suspect turned himself in after a warrant was issued by Suffolk County Juvenile Court, according to Boston police.
Charges include assault with intent to murder, firearm violations, and discharging a firearm within 500 feet of a building.

title: Alex Cora calls Terry Francona a ‘Hall of Fame manager’
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Alex Cora praised Terry Francona as a 'Hall of Fame manager' during the Red Sox season opener against the Reds.
Terry Francona managed the Boston Red Sox from 2004-2011, leading them to two World Series titles.
The Red Sox lost two of three games in their season-opening series against the Cincinnati Reds.

title: Victim tied up by armed intruder during Beverly home invasion, police say
author: John Waller
bullets: An armed intruder broke into a Beverly home on Paine Avenue Saturday morning, tying up the sole occupant.
Several items, including a vehicle, were stolen from the residence; the stolen vehicle was later found.
The victim was treated for non-life-threatening injuries at Beverly Hospital and released; the investigation continues.
